question_title: What will the Ifo Business Climate Index level (points) for Germany be for February 2026?
summary: The Ifo Business Climate Index for February 2026 is forecast based on the January 2026 reading of 87.6 points (unchanged from December 2025). The index peaked at 88.8 in August 2025 and has trended flat-to-slightly-lower since. Key factors: (1) ZEW economic sentiment surged to a 4-year high of 59.6, which often leads Ifo improvement; (2) fiscal expansion and manufacturing stabilization provide modest upside; (3) Trump tariff uncertainty (Greenland tariffs proposed then walked back, ongoing trade threats) creates downside risk; (4) the expectations sub-component declined slightly in January. Monthly volatility is ~0.66 points historically, widened to ~1.0 for current geopolitical uncertainty. Monte Carlo simulation with a 10% tariff shock scenario produces a median around 87.4-87.5 with moderate left-skew.

factors.description: January 2026 Ifo at 87.6, unchanged from December - establishes strong anchor point for February forecast
ZEW Economic Sentiment surged to 59.6 in January 2026 (4-year high) - ZEW often leads Ifo by 1-2 months, suggesting possible improvement
German fiscal expansion expected to boost domestic demand in 2026 (Goldman forecasts 1.1% growth), manufacturing stabilizing
Trump tariff uncertainty persists - Greenland tariffs walked back Jan 21 but could return; 25% threat for June 2026 still looming
Expectations sub-component declined slightly in January (89.5 vs 89.7), suggesting mild pessimism trend
Historical monthly change SD is only 0.66 points - extreme moves are uncommon, index is mean-reverting
